text: This is a deliberately **very long** task title meant purely to demonstrate that YATA correctly *wraps* long task text across multiple lines instead of clipping or overflowing the window: it describes, in unnecessary and repetitive detail, a plan to reorganize the entire home office, including sorting through several boxes of old cables, donating the **CRT monitor** nobody needs anymore, alphabetizing the bookshelf by author surname, backing up every old hard drive to a `NAS` before recycling it, framing the `git commit -m "initial"` printout as a joke, testing whether ~~procrastination~~ focus really is a renewable resource, and finally treating myself to a coffee once the whole thing is [actually done](https://example.com/definitely-done) instead of just marked done.
Draft the **quarterly review** slides for Monday
Call the plumber about the *leaking* kitchen faucet
Pick up dry cleaning (~~Tuesday~~ now Wednesday)
Read the `RFC-822` spec sections on header folding
Reply to Alex re: [budget spreadsheet](https://example.com/budget)
Book flights for the **conference** in October
Renew *car* insurance before it lapses
Submit expense report for last week's trip
Fix the `NullPointerException` in the login flow
Water the plants on the balcony
Send a birthday card to **Grandma**
Order a new office chair (~~decided against it~~)
Sign up for the pottery class
Migrate the blog to *Hugo* -- shelved
Finish reading **Project Hail Mary**
Deploy the `v2.3.1` hotfix to production
Review Sam's PR on the *auth* module
Clean out the garage
Pay the electricity bill
Weekend camping trip (~~cancelled~~ due to rain)
